scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a standard audience-services case only when patron, booking, entitlement, and accommodation records aligned.
Missing patron, event, and booking prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative service guidance.
Unsupported donor and fee exceptions were treated as approval-bound policy exceptions and routed rather than executed in-lane.
A patron communication that turned into venue-entry and screening approval was handed to the correct owner without over-resolving the issue.
Safety-sensitive accommodation and route questions triggered venue-operations escalation rather than unsupported reassurance.
Suspicious will-call and account-change signals were preserved and routed to fraud or loss-prevention instead of being cleared for patron convenience.
The lane triaged a mixed opening-night queue by request class and authority boundary without flattening it into generic customer-service scripting.
The in-scope audience-services scenario preserved arts-specific terminology such as season package, donor presale, companion seating, assistive listening, and late seating.
Conflicting public guidance and tenant policy triggered escalation rather than false-confidence synthesis on credits, refunds, and accommodations.
Unsupported asks for age-rule overrides, protected inventory changes, recording rights, and off-policy comps were rejected cleanly.
Booking, payment, and check-in mismatches were summarized with explicit boundaries instead of forcing a discretionary fix.
Tenant-specific donor and late-seating overlays were applied as stricter runtime constraints without losing the public baseline.
Repeat failure handling escalated unresolved accessible seating and exchange issues with preserved blocker history instead of another generic apology.

notes: All 13 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 13 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
The package stays inside audience services and does not claim pricing authority, venue-security command, fraud adjudication, legal interpretation, or discretionary exception approval.
